instruction: Extract structured information from the following job description. Return valid JSON with these fields: title, company, location, work_model, seniority, required_skills (list), nice_to_have (list), salary, language.
input:
AI SWE
PUNKU.AI
Cottbus, Germany
Full-time
Entry-level (Berufseinstieg)

About the Company:
PUNKU.AI is the leading AI Agent platform for Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (SMEs) globally. Mission focuses on addressing workforce shortages in SMEs through AI-powered solutions that bridge critical skill gaps and enable business competitiveness.

About the Role:
Build the AI Agent platform enabling SME customers to create their own AI Agents. Design, develop, and maintain essential platform components.

Key Responsibilities:
- Build intuitive interfaces and abstractions enabling customers to build their own AI Agents
- Design, develop, and maintain essential platform components using Python and TypeScript
- Produce clean, scalable, and well-documented code
- Stay informed about emerging technologies and apply innovations to business challenges

Required Skills:
- Background in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent field
- Self-directed learning capability with efficient project completion
- Proactive thinking and independent execution without supervision
- Strong proficiency in TypeScript and Python
- AWS cloud infrastructure experience

Nice-to-Have:
- LangChain familiarity
- Entrepreneurial mindset with future founder ambitions

Language: Not specified (German posting, but technical English likely)
Note: 200+ applications received

instruction: Extract structured information from the following job description. Return valid JSON with these fields: title, company, location, work_model, seniority, required_skills (list), nice_to_have (list), salary, language.
input:
AI / LLM Application Engineer (m/f/d)
OMMAX
Munich, Germany (Hybrid — offices in Munich, London, Amsterdam, Milan, Berlin)
Full-time, Permanent
Entry-level (Berufseinstieg)

About the Company:
OMMAX is a digital strategy and technology consultancy helping companies unlock value through AI, data, and digital transformation. Works with enterprise clients across Europe.

About the Role:
Build and deploy AI-powered applications for enterprise clients. Design scalable LLM-based solutions including RAG architectures, agentic workflows, and AI-integrated frontend experiences.

Key Responsibilities:
- Build and deploy AI-powered applications for enterprise clients
- Design and implement RAG architectures and agentic workflows
- Develop scalable LLM-based solutions with production deployment
- Build frontend experiences with React/Next.js integrated with AI backends
- Deploy and manage cloud infrastructure for AI applications
- Implement enterprise security and IAM for AI systems

Required Skills:
- Python (production-level experience)
- LLM frameworks (LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex)
- RAG architectures
- Cloud deployment (AWS, Azure, GCP)
- Docker and CI/CD
- React/Next.js
- Enterprise security and IAM
- Agentic/workflow system design

Language: Fluent English required; additional local language skills a plus

Benefits: Hybrid work across European offices, enterprise client exposure, AI-focused team

instruction: Extract structured information from the following job description. Return valid JSON with these fields: title, company, location, work_model, seniority, required_skills (list), nice_to_have (list), salary, language.
input:
Agentic AI Software Engineer
Machine Learning Reply
Munich, Germany (Hybrid: client site / Reply office / remote)
Full-time, Permanent
Entry-level, English-speaking workplace

About the Company:
Machine Learning Reply is part of the Reply network with over 8,000 employees. Extensive expertise in Data Science across all key industries of DAX100 clients. Provides customized end-to-end data science solutions.

About the Role:
Combines strong software engineering expertise with hands-on experience in modern AI systems, including agentic AI and generative AI applications. Support clients in designing and building intelligent, production-ready systems that leverage LLMs, autonomous agents, and scalable cloud architectures.

Key Responsibilities:
- Design, build, and deploy scalable, production-grade systems on cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure)
- Develop and operate agentic AI systems including multi-step workflows, tool integration, and autonomous decision-making
- Lead end-to-end implementation of AI-driven features from PoC to production deployment
- Build high-performance APIs and backend services using FastAPI, Flask, or Spring Boot
- Collaborate with client teams on AI solution design

Required Skills:
- Completed university studies with strong quantitative/technical background (CS, Data Science, Engineering)
- Solid programming in at least two languages: Python, Java, Rust, or JavaScript
- Practical experience deploying ML/AI models into production
- Experience with SQL and NoSQL databases, data modeling
- Cloud platform experience (AWS, GCP, or Azure)

Nice-to-Have:
- Experience with LLM frameworks and agentic workflows
- Containerization (Docker, Kubernetes)
- Client-facing communication skills

Language: English (English-speaking workplace)

Logicc is a European AI platform startup emphasizing highest data protection standards with all data stored exclusively in Germany and encrypted throughout.

Design, develop, and maintain AI features from concept through release. Build and improve RAG systems, develop agentic systems, and create infrastructure for model evaluation, comparison, monitoring, and continuous improvement.

Nice-to-Have:

- React, Next.js, and Tailwind expertise

instruction: Extract structured information from the following job description. Return valid JSON with these fields: title, company, location, work_model, seniority, required_skills (list), nice_to_have (list), salary, language.
input:
Applied AI Engineer (f/m/x) - MAIA

Location: Leipzig, Germany (Remote-first, fully remote within Germany)
Salary: €75,000 - €85,000 annually

Key Responsibilities:
The role focuses on building production-grade AI systems. Core duties include developing RAG pipelines, retrieval strategies, agentic workflows, and LLM integrations that function reliably in production environments. The engineer designs and ships features from conception through production deployment, with emphasis on backend and AI functionality. Additional responsibilities include constructing evaluation frameworks for LLM features and exploring emerging technologies to advance team capabilities.

Required Technical Skills:
- Strong backend engineering in TypeScript or strongly-typed languages (Go, Java, Rust)
- Production REST API development experience
- Advanced SQL proficiency, preferably PostgreSQL
- Hands-on LLM application building with RAG system exposure
- Commercial experience with LLM frameworks and vector databases
- Production system debugging using observability tools (Grafana, Sentry, PostHog)
- Cloud and DevOps fundamentals
- Secure development best practices
- Fluent English; German is advantageous

Required Work Style Competencies:
Candidates should demonstrate strong ownership mentality, collaborative interpersonal skills, active AI exploration, pragmatic problem-solving, business-focused prioritization, startup environment comfort, and clear stakeholder communication across technical and non-technical audiences.

Nice-to-Have Skills:
- Supabase experience
- Document analysis and retrieval preprocessing pipelines
- Automated LLM feature evaluation and testing
- LLM tracing and analytics

Experience Level: Professional/Experienced

Hiring Timeline: Target completion by May 2026 (three-stage process: initial call, technical interview, leadership conversation)

instruction: Extract structured information from the following job description. Return valid JSON with these fields: title, company, location, work_model, seniority, required_skills (list), nice_to_have (list), salary, language.
input:
Forward Deployed Engineer, Applied AI
Anthropic
Munich, Germany

About Anthropic
Anthropic's mission is to create reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. We want AI to be safe and beneficial for our users and for society as a whole. Our team is a quickly growing group of committed researchers, engineers, policy experts, and business leaders working together to build beneficial AI systems.

As a member of the Applied AI team at Anthropic, you will be a Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) who embeds directly with our most strategic customers to drive transformational AI adoption. You will collaborate closely with customer teams to ship advanced AI applications that solve real world business problems. Our FDEs engage with customers to accelerate the adoption of existing products and create new applications built on our models.

Working closely with our Post-Sales, Product, and Engineering teams, you'll combine engineering expertise, an understanding of frontier AI applications, and customer-facing skills to understand customer workflows and develop innovative solutions that address complex business challenges while maintaining our high standards for safety and reliability.

You will sit at the frontier of enterprise AI deployments and serve as one of our founding FDEs who helps to shape our forward-deployed motion. We expect our FDEs to operate autonomously, thrive under ambiguity, and represent Anthropic at the highest level in customer environments. This is a significant responsibility: you'll play a key role in championing our mission in the enterprise.

Responsibilities
- Work within customer systems to build production applications with Claude models, ensuring that these products meet customer requirements.
- Deliver technical artifacts for customers like MCP servers, sub-agents, and agent skills that will be used in production workflows.
- Provide white glove deployment support for Anthropic products in enterprise environments.
- Identify and codify repeatable deployment patterns and contribute insights back to our Product and Engineering teams.
- Maintain strong knowledge of the latest developments in LLM capabilities, implementation patterns, and AI product development stacks.
- Build long term relationships with customers and proactively identify new opportunities for AI deployment throughout the lifecycle of an engagement.
- Travel frequently (25-50%) to customer sites to build in person with customers.
- Be a champion for Anthropic's mission in the field.

You May Be a Good Fit If You Have
- 4+ years of experience in a technical, customer facing role such as Forward Deployed Engineer, or as a Software Engineer with consulting experience. Former technical founders are also encouraged to apply.
- Native or C1 German speaker with fluent English proficiency
- Production experience with LLMs including advanced prompt engineering, agent development, evaluation frameworks, and deployment at scale.
- Strong programming skills with proficiency in Python (and ideally in one or more additional languages like TypeScript, Java, etc) and experience shipping production applications
- High agency with an ability to navigate ambiguity present in complex organizations.
- High cooperation mindset for cross-organizational collaboration, balancing competing priorities with integrity.
- Passion for advancing safe, beneficial AI systems through creative technical applications.
- Strong communication skills to conduct discovery with customers and to convey technical concepts to diverse stakeholders while maintaining a low ego and collaborative approach.
- A background in financial services, healthcare/life sciences, or another enterprise vertical is a plus.
- Experience with enterprise IT systems and/or AI deployment patterns is a plus.

Annual Salary: 205,000 - 220,000 EUR
Visa sponsorship available
Hybrid: 25% in-office at Munich
output:
{
  "title": "Forward Deployed Engineer, Applied AI",
  "company": "Anthropic",
  "location": "Munich, Germany",
  "work_model": "hybrid",
  "seniority": "senior",
  "required_skills": [
    "Python",
    "TypeScript",
    "LLM deployment",
    "prompt engineering",
    "agent development",
    "evaluation frameworks"
  ],
  "nice_to_have": [
    "financial services",
    "healthcare",
    "enterprise IT systems"
  ],
  "salary": "205,000-220,000 EUR",
  "language": "German (C1) + English"
}

input:
ML Engineer - Small Language Models
fastino.ai
Palo Alto, California (Hybrid or Remote)
Full-time

About the Company:
fastino.ai builds specialized, efficient AI. Their GLiNER family of open source models has been downloaded more than 5 million times and is used by companies such as NVIDIA, Meta, and Airbnb. Raised $25M backed by Microsoft, Khosla Ventures, Insight Partners, and executives from Github and Docker. Team includes alumni from Google Research, Apple, Stanford, and Cambridge.

About the Role:
Design, build, and deploy the critical small language models foundational to fastino's product. Own the full lifecycle of state-of-the-art models from prototyping and data analysis to deployment, monitoring, and continuous improvement.

Requirements:
- 2+ years of hands-on experience in AI/ML engineering roles
- Demonstrated proficiency with LLMs
- Track record of applying AI/ML techniques to solve complex, unstructured problems
- Comfortable working across the stack from prompt engineering and vector DB tuning to Kubernetes deployment and API design

Tech Stack:
- Python, ML frameworks
- LLMs, Small Language Models
- Vector databases
- Kubernetes
- Cloud infrastructure

Language: English
